plan_history.intent_description: R3 has no BGP configured so its BGP session(s) are down; configure BGP on R3 and configure corresponding neighbors on R1 and R2 so the BGP adjacencies become UP and routes are exchanged.
plan_history.problem: R3 has no BGP process or neighbors configured; peers R1 and R2 also lack BGP. Missing inputs: intended ASNs, explicit neighbor IPs, and any import/export policies. Assumptions made: R3 ASN=65003, R2 ASN=65002, R1 ASN=65001; R3 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.9) peers with R2 at 10.0.0.8; R3 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.6) peers with R1 at 10.0.0.5; no route-maps/prefix-lists applied initially.
plan_history.fix_summary: Create BGP process on router3 (AS 65003) and configure neighbor statements on router3, router2, and router1 using the assumed ASNs and link IPs so eBGP adjacencies form and loopback/connected routes can be advertised.

plan_history.intent_description: R3 has no BGP process and R1/R2 have no BGP neighbor statements, so BGP adjacencies are DOWN and routes are not exchanged. Goal: configure BGP on R3 and add neighbors on R1 and R2 so adjacencies come UP and R3's loopback 203.0.113.1/32 is advertised.
plan_history.problem: R3 has no 'router bgp' configuration; R1 and R2 lack neighbor statements for R3. BGP sessions are down; R3's loopback (203.0.113.1/32) is not being advertised via BGP.
plan_history.fix_summary: Create BGP process on router3 (assume AS 65003, router-id 3.3.3.3) advertising 203.0.113.1/32 and configure neighbors on R1 and R2 (assume R1 AS 65001, R2 AS 65002). Assumed link IPs: R3->R1=10.0.0.9, R1->R3=10.0.0.10; R3->R2=10.0.0.6, R2->R3=10.0.0.5. If actual AS numbers or neighbor IPs differ, adjust accordingly.

remaining_issues: No BGP process configured on R3 (no 'router bgp' section found).
R1 and R2 have no BGP neighbor statements configured for R3 (no 'neighbor' entries found).
